content.problemStatement:
There are NNN people in the vaccination queue, Chef is standing on the PthP^{th}Pth position from the front of the queue. It takes XXX minutes to vaccinate a child and YYY minutes to vaccinate an elderly person. Assume Chef is an elderly person.

You are given a binary array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of length NNN, where Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 denotes there is an elderly person standing on the ithi^{th}ith position of the queue, Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 denotes there is a child standing on the ithi^{th}ith position of the queue. You are also given the three integers P,X,YP, X, YP,X,Y. Find the number of minutes after which Chef's vaccination will be completed.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each test case contains four space-separated integers N,P,X,YN, P, X, YN,P,X,Y.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integer A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line the number of minutes after which Chef's vaccination will be completed.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains four space‑separated integers N, P, X and Y.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN where Ai is 0 for a child and 1 for an elderly person. It is guaranteed that AP = 1 (Chef is elderly).
Positions are 1‑indexed, and Chef stands at position P.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total number of minutes after which Chef's vaccination is completed.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Queue = [child, Chef, child, elderly]. Time = 3 (child) + 2 (Chef) = 5.\nTest case 2: Chef is at the front, time = 3.\nTest case 3: Chef is last, time = 2+2+2 = 6.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need the time taken for the first P people in the queue (including Chef). Iterate i from 1 to P, add X minutes if Ai = 0 (child) otherwise add Y minutes. The sum is the answer. This is O(P) per test case, which is O(N) in the worst case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the queue.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array.
